name: RenderLit3D
description: Universal 3D volume raymarcher with advanced lighting
defaultProgram:
search synth3d, filter3d, render

noise3d()
.renderLit3d(specularIntensity: 2, shininess: 256)
.write(o0)

volumeSize._note: Always inherited from the upstream volume effect (inheritsVolumeSize); no UI control. Drives the atlas texel addressing (atlasTexel = vx, vy + vz*volumeSize), the central-difference eps (2/volumeSize), and the raymarch stepSize (1.5/volumeSize).

invert._note: WGSL/GLSL test `invert == 1`. HLSL int uniform set 1/0; tested == 1.

render._note: WGSL packs uniforms in a std140 Uniforms struct (resolution, time, threshold, volumeSize, invert, shape, orbitSpeed, cameraPosition, bgAlpha, bgColor, diffuseIntensity, lightDirection, specularIntensity, diffuseColor, shininess, specularColor, rimIntensity, ambientColor, rimPower, tileOffset, fullResolution; 144 bytes). The HLSL port sets every param as an individual named uniform by its reference `uniform` name via MaterialPropertyBlock; engine globals resolution/time/tileOffset/fullResolution come from NMFullscreen. Values are identical (reference 07 §3).

passes.name: render
passes._role: RAYMARCH (fullscreen consumer, MRT drawBuffers:2). Renders at SCREEN resolution. Reads the upstream vol-tier atlas (volumeCache <- inputTex3d) via integer texel Load with MANUAL trilinear filtering (8-corner fetch + lerp), raymarches it (intersect cube/sphere bounds, fixed-step march stepSize=1.5/volumeSize up to MAX_STEPS=256, then 8-step bisection refine on the threshold crossing), shades the isosurface (Blinn-Phong diffuse/specular + Fresnel rim + ambient, gamma 1/2.2). Writes SV_Target0 (color->outputTex) = lit RGB+alpha, SV_Target1 (geoOut->screenGeoBuffer) = (normal*0.5+0.5, depth=dist/10). analyticalGeo<-inputGeo is bound but the body never samples it. NM_FragCoord yields screen pixel coords; uv = ((fragCoord+tileOffset) - 0.5*fullRes)/fullRes.y (divide by HEIGHT).

inputTex3d._role: INPUT vol-tier atlas surface (reference 04 §8). Supplied by the upstream synth3d/filter3d generator (its outputTex3d). 2D RenderTexture volumeSize x volumeSize^2 (default 64 x 4096 == 64 slices of 64x64), rgba16f LINEAR. Bound to sampler `volumeCache`; raymarched (read via Load, point fetch). Atlas (x,y) <- voxel (vx,vy,vz): atlasTexel = (vx, vy + vz*volumeSize). NOT created by this effect — exposed as outputTex3d for chaining.

inputGeo._role: INPUT geo-tier atlas surface (reference 04 §8: xyz=normal, w=depth/density). Supplied by the upstream generator (its outputGeo). Bound to sampler `analyticalGeo` but NEVER sampled by the shader body (normals are recomputed via finite-difference calcNormal / calcBoundaryNormal). Declared for binding parity only.

screenGeoBuffer._role: OUTPUT geo buffer (screen-space, 2D, NOT a vol atlas). Screen W x H, rgba16f LINEAR. Written by SV_Target1 (geoOut): xyz = world-space normal*0.5+0.5, w = depth = hit.dist/MAX_DIST (MAX_DIST=10). Background pixels write neutral normal (0.5,0.5,1.0... actually (0,0,1)*0.5+0.5 = (0.5,0.5,1.0)) and depth=1.0. Exposed as outputGeo for downstream consumers.

render3d._comment: FIRST 3D / RENDER-TIER RAYMARCH CONSUMER port. Single fullscreen pass ('render') at SCREEN resolution, MRT (drawBuffers:2). Reads an upstream vol-tier atlas (inputTex3d, default 64x4096 rgba16f LINEAR) and raymarches it into a lit 2D image, writing color (outputTex) + a screen-space geo buffer (screenGeoBuffer: xyz=normal*0.5+0.5, w=depth). The geo-tier inputGeo is bound but unused by the body. No volume WRITE, no feedback, no geometry/scatter, no loop. PORTED FROM WGSL (golden rule #1): the WGSL bisection refine loop uses a SEPARATE `bisectPrevField` accumulator (the GLSL mutates `prevField` directly — divergent); we follow the WGSL. textureLoad -> Texture2D.Load(int3(coord,0)) point fetch; trilinear filtering is MANUAL (8-corner fetch + lerp). uv divides by fullRes.y (HEIGHT). Loop bounds: MAX_STEPS=256, bisection j<8 inclusive. No nm_mod/pcg/random used.

raymarchParams.bounds: cube (shape 0): ray-box [-1,1]^3; sphere (shape 1): unit sphere at origin
